title: carex longii subsp. meridionalis
intro: Carex longii subsp. meridionalis is a subspecies of sedge, a type of aquatic grass-like plant. It is also known as Meridional Long's Sedge or Long's Sedge, which is native to eastern North America. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be used to treat wounds and skin conditions. Carex longii subsp. meridionalis has been used in herbal preparations such as salves, ointments, and infusions, which may be beneficial for skin health, wound healing, and immune system support.
